title: DynaVerif: Decentralized Adversarial Proof Generation and Dynamic Axiom Discovery for Multi-Agent Formal Verification
failure_mode:
### Baseline Failure Mode
The strongest baseline is the multi-agent discovery system from *Discovering mathematical concepts through a multi-agent system*, which uses a fixed Lean verifier and static axiom set. (a) It fails on domains lacking a pre-built formal environment (e.g., non-associative magmas, paraconsistent logics) because no Lean library exists. (b) Root cause Y: the centralized, static verification component prevents the formal environment from co-evolving with agent discoveries. (c) Empirical anchor: the paper reports that all discovered theorems are confined to Lean’s pre-defined theory, yielding zero verified conjectures when applied to a new axiom system (e.g., commutative magmas with identity). 

### Why Our Method Addresses Y
X fails because Y; we address Y by introducing W: adversarial role rotation and dynamic axiom updates. (a) We change variable V from a static axiom set (S₀) to a learnable artifact updated through collective proof failures (S evolves each round). (b) Mechanism: by rotating agents as provers and verifiers, the system treats the formal environment as co-learnable – verifiers produce counterexamples that trigger axiom revisions, enabling adaptation without a pre-specified verifier. (c) Removing W (specifically, removing the verifier role’s ability to update axioms) would revert to a static environment, re-introducing Y.

### Predicted Gain
On the Equational Theories Project dataset D (200 equational laws from magmas to groups), primary metric M is the number of verified non-trivial conjectures (higher better). Baseline achieves ~45 verified conjectures; our method achieves ~75, with k ≥ 30. Gain is largest on a subset of 50 non-associative laws where baseline’s fixed verifier has no pre-loaded inference rules (Y most severe); gain is smallest on the 50 group-theoretic laws where Lean’s library already covers all needed rules (Y does not apply).

### Falsifier
If the number of verified conjectures M on the non-associative subset (where Y is most severe) does not exceed the baseline’s value (≈5) by at least 10, the causal claim that W addresses Y is refuted, regardless of overall gains on other subsets. This condition ensures the specific mechanism of dynamic axiom adaptation is necessary and sufficient.
